title: API Proof
purpose: Verify an API contract through an actual request/response, fixture, or documented blocker with honest evidence labels.
trigger: Run when a task claims endpoint behavior, integration contract, webhook handling, or readback API behavior.
inputs: server start command or target environment
allowedActions: start local server or use approved target environment
record status code and redacted response fields
verify negative cases when in scope
forbiddenActions: send real provider/SMS/payment requests without explicit approval
claim production direct proof from localhost
mutate production data unless explicitly allowed
gates: request command or test name recorded
response status and key fields recorded
negative or error path checked when relevant

evidence.direct: Approved target API was called and returned the expected behavior.
evidence.proxy: Generated client, contract test, or logs support the behavior without a live request.
evidence.local: Local server, fixture, or unit-level API proof only.
evidence.blocked: Auth unavailable, endpoint unavailable, unsafe side effect, or provider access missing.

escalation: Ask before calling third-party providers or production endpoints.
Stop if proof requires secrets that are not available.
Escalate when the endpoint has side effects that need product approval.
